gloss:
There exist measurable read functions and a measurable set whose closure under 'reads the same' (its saturation) is not measurable — an explicit classical analytic, non-Borel counterexample. This refutes, as stated, the conjecture that every bisimilarity on a standard Borel space admits a Borel selector.

**Cites** `WMSpec.Transform.exists_saturate_not_measurableSet` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The corpus lemma is a bare measure-theoretic existence fact; the thesis layer names it as the in-corpus refuter of a specific stated conjecture (the unrestricted Borel-selector claim) and files it as the disproof half of a conjecture pair, turning a lemma into a recorded refutation.

statement.pretty: ∃ T B, (∀ (i : ℕ), Measurable (T i)) ∧ MeasurableSet B ∧ ¬MeasurableSet (WMSpec.Transform.saturate T id B)

title: Eidometry.Thesis.counterfactual_ct3b_refuter
